Lee Valley White Water Rafting Welcome to Epping Forest Steeped in history, full of character and jewelled with green spaces, every season offers all the right ingredient for a short break in the Epping Forest District. Find places to eat and drink, events and activities and offers and promotions on this site and on our free Discover App . Epping Forest forms a direct green link between London and rural Essex and lends its name to a market town and surrounding district that never fails to surprise and delight visitors who discover its many attractions. And all just a walk, ride or tube journey away from the Capital. Immersed in history, the ancient market town of Waltham Abbey is the resting place of King Harold and home to the Epping Forest District Museum where you can learn about the district's rich heritage, and the Royal Gunpowder Mills where you can discover the secret history of gunpowder production and rocket development. Just a few minutes away, the world class Lee Valley White Water Centre offers adrenaline-pumping white water adventure. Alternatively, for a gentler taste of the outdoors, head for Epping Forest , with ancient trees, sunlit glades and a host of events throughout the year. Visit Queen Elizabeth's Hunting Lodge or explore the remains of two large Iron Age forts. Alternatively, enjoy English countryside at its best along the Essex Way which begins right outside Epping Station at the end of the Central Line. Lee Valley Regional Park , running along the western edge of the Epping Forest district, offers much to the leisure visitor and Lee Valley Park Farms plus Ashlyns Farm at North Weald are popular family days out. Just outside Ongar , Greensted’s 11th Century Church is famed as the oldest wooden church in the world whilst at Copped Hall , Shakespeare’s Midsummer Night’s Dream was first performed. Combine your visit with lunch in the leafy market towns of Epping or Ongar, two destinations linked by the Epping Ongar Heritage Railway , who can take you even further on one of their classic double-decker buses. Bargain hunters head for North Weald Airfield , host to a weekly outdoor market held Saturdays and Bank Holidays on a former Battle of Britain site, still active today with historic aircraft in the skies and events throughout the year. The Airfield Museum is another must-see for enthusiasts. For the ultimate retail therapy, especially if bling’s your thing, head for Loughton and Buckhurst Hill where you’ll find shops full of the fashion and designer goods that inspired the TOWIE look. You’ll also find plenty of café’s and bars to relax and admire your purchases or plan your evening at top night spots or fine dining in one of the district’s many acclaimed restaurants. Don’t miss the chance to gain wisdom from these revered figures. Keep an eye on our schedule page for updates on upcoming visitors. Gauranga Darshan das Author, Speaker Dedicated member of ISKCON, he has made significant contributions through various roles, including regional leader and mentor and devotional education. Known for his deep understanding of Vedic scriptures, he is also an accomplished author of several books that explore and elucidate key aspects of Krishna consciousness. His work in organising seminars and outreach programs has been pivotal in expanding ISKCON’s impact, while his writings continue to inspire and guide devotees worldwide. HG Gauranga Das ISKCON GBC, Speaker B.Tech graduate from IIT Bombay, holds numerous key roles within ISKCON, as GBC member, trustee of ISKCON GBC College, and director of the award-winning Govardhan Ecovillage. He also serves as Co-President of ISKCON Chowpatty and administrative director for the Bhaktivedanta Research Centre, Kolkata. Renowned for dynamic presentations, he has delivered over 10,000 discourses on scriptures such as Srimad Bhagavatam and Bhagavad Gita, making spirituality both accessible and compelling. HH Bhanu Swami ISKCON Sannyasi Graduated in Oriental Fine Arts History from the University of British Columbia, Canada, joined the Hare Krishna movement in 1970 and was initiated by Srila Prabhupada in 1971. Taking sannyasa in 1984, he has become an authority on Deity worship within ISKCON and has been a GBC member since 2003, overseeing South India and co-GBC for Japan. He has translated 15 key Gaudiya Vaishnava texts and is involved in temple projects in Chennai and Tokyo, while sharing his deep spiritual knowledge globally. HH Mahavishnu Swami ISKCON Sannyasi, Guru He was born on February 10, 1945, in Birmingham, UK, and graduated with honors in Fine Arts from the Bath Academy of Art. Initiated by Srila Prabhupada in 1971, he became a full-time temple renunciate that same year. He has traveled extensively in India and globally to support and fund ISKCON’s temple projects, including significant contributions to temples in Bangladesh, Nepal, and India. His dedicated efforts have played a crucial role in expanding the reach of Krishna Consciousness worldwide. HH Sivarama Swami ISKCON Sannyasi, Guru Born in Budapest in 1949, was initiated into ISKCON by A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada in 1973 and later became a sannyasi in 1979. After serving ISKCON in Canada, the USA, and the UK, he focused on leading the movement in Hungary, Romania, and Turkey from 2003. His leadership has significantly advanced Krishna consciousness in Hungary, making ISKCON one of the country’s top religious groups. He continues to be a pivotal figure in guiding and expanding the movement in these regions. HH B.B. Govinda Swami ISKCON Sanyasi Bhakti Bhringa Govinda Swami joined ISKCON in Buffalo, NY, in 1971, receiving initiation from Srila Prabhupada that same year. He served in ISKCON centers in Buffalo and Toronto before moving to Vrindavan in 1977. From 1979 to 1987, he served in ISKCON temples in Greece, Israel, Italy, and France. Returning to Vrindavan, he established the Food For Life program. Known for his lively congregational chanting events, he became a GBC member in 2000 and currently serves as zonal secretary for various regions. HH Janananda Goswami ISKCON Sanyasi Joined ISKCON in London and was initiated by Srila Prabhupada in 1972. Janananda Goswami Maharaj has served in various places around the world including, UK and Europe, Africa, India, Malaysia, Philippines, Australia, New Zealand and America. Maharaj is well known for his ecstatic kirtans, harinam and book distribution in the line of Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu. Maharaj’s quick wit, humility and loving personal nature is very uplifting and inspires us all to take shelter of the Lotus feet of Srila Prabhupada and Lord Krishna. HH Indradyumna Swami ISKCON Sanyasi A disciple of Srila Prabhupada, Indradyumna Swami serves as a traveling preacher and guru in the Gaudiya tradition. He travels globally, teaching the Bhagavad-gita and leading chanting of the Hare Krishna maha mantra. His annual activities include coordinating the Festival in Polish Baltic coast from June to September and leading pilgrimages to Vrindavan during the sacred month of Kartik. He documents his experiences in “The Diary of a Traveling Preacher,” now in its eighth volume, published annually as a book. HH Jayadvaita Swami ISKCON Sanyasi Disciple of Srila Prabhupada, Jayadvaita Swami serves as an editor, publisher and teacher within ISKCON. He played a significant role in editing Prabhupada’s books, received initiation in 1968, and co-founded the Vrindaban Institute for Higher Education in 1987. Since 1988, he has directed the Bhaktivedanta Book Trust in Africa. From 1991 to 1998, he was the editor-in-chief of BTG magazine. Although he doesn’t initiate disciples, he contributes widely to publish and teaches Krishna consciousness philosophy. HH Devamrita Swami ISKCON Sanyasi Devamrita Swami’s journey from a devout Christian upbringing to immersion in Krishna consciousness began at Yale University in 1972 after encountering Srila Prabhupada’s teachings. Since joining ISKCON in 1973, he has dedicated over three decades to spreading the message of Bhagavad-gita and Srimad-Bhagavatam worldwide. As an author and member of ISKCON’s Governing Body, he inspires spiritual seekers globally and advocates for socially-conscious approaches to spirituality, sustainability, and economics. HH Bh. Caitanya Swami ISKCON Sanyasi Originally from New Zealand, Bhakti Caitanya Swami joined the Hare Krishna movement in London, early in 1973. A year before, as a philosophy student at Auckland University, he had first seen his future spiritual master walking across the campus with his followers. Srila Prabhupada initiated him later in 1973, giving him the name Raghubir Dasa. Eleven years later in Mayapur, the order of sannyasa was awarded to Bhakti Caitanya Swami, who is an initiating spiritual master for many devotees around the world at a present time. HH S.B. Keshava Swami ISKCON Sanyasi Svayam Bhagavan Keshava Swami is a spiritual author, mentor, and teacher. After graduating from UCL in 2002, he embraced full-time monastic life to deepen his spirituality and share timeless principles. For over two decades at ISKCON UK’s headquarters, Bhaktivedanta Manor, he pioneered the School of Bhakti, led monastic training programs, authored ten books, and accepted lifetime renunciation vows in 2022. Now a globe-trotter, he teaches in various settings, aiming to spread ancient wisdom. HG Nitai Sevini Devi dasi Speaker, Preacher Since 1984 she became closely connected to ISKCON Secunderabad. She received initiation from HH Jayapataka Swami in 1998 in Atlanta, USA. With degrees in Commerce, Education & Management of Voluntary Organizations, she currently is the Principal of ISKCON’s Divine Touch School. Alongside her husband, H.G. Samba Das, President of ISKCON Visakhapatnam, she manages various responsibilities and initiated the successful Bhakti Yoga Residential Summer camp in Visakhapatnam for school children. HG Bhuta Bhavana das Speaker, Preacher Originally from London, UK, he graduated from the London School of Economics with a degree in Politics and Philosophy, later earning a Master’s from Birkbeck University of London. With a background in Motivation and Leadership Consulting, he contributes to various initiatives through workshops. Known for his speaking and preaching skills, he addresses topics like “Leadership from Within,” “Empowerment through Yoga and Meditation,” and “Stress Management.” He is a senior disciple of HH Bhakti Tirtha Swami. Read More Register TO History 55 Years Of Service In 1968, three American married couples – Gurudasa & Yamuna, Syamasundara & Malati (along with their baby daughter, Sarasvati), Mukunda & Janaki – at the request of their spiritual master, Srila Prabhupada, left San Francisco for London to start a new temple. Eager to establish Krishna consciousness in London to please their spiritual master, they met with Srila Prabhupada in Montreal to discuss plans for London, get his blessings, and practice kirtana under his direction. They boarded for London just based on the faith on the words of Srila Prabhupada. The bliss and the struggle just started to unfold without knowing what was waiting for them… MORE ABOUT OUR FOUNDER Founder-Acarya His divine grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada ISKCON was founded in 1966 in New York by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada, affectionately known as Srila Prabhupada by his followers. With great effort and determination, at the age of 69 (when most people are retired), he journeyed from Kolkata to New York by cargo ship hoping to help the people of the Western world to reconnect with their spiritual essence. He sought to pass on the ancient teachings of bhakti-yoga and demonstrated how to practically apply this knowledge to live a happy and fulfilling life. Srila Prabhupada has unlocked the secrets and sacred spiritual knowledge in the Vedic tradition and made them accessible to everyone. His “Bhagavad-Gita As It Is” is the largest selling edition of the Bhagavad-Gita in the Western world and translated in over 76 languages. MORE ABOUT HIS JOURNEY 55 Years Later Bhakti still abundantly flows out of Soho For over 50 years, the Radha-Krishna Temple has served as a spiritual oasis for thousands of Londoners and visitors from all over the world. Whether you are looking for a peaceful space to reflect, an opportunity to learn more about spirituality and wisdom from the East, or just curious to try our world-famous vegetarian cuisine – a warm welcome awaits you. Ramanatha-sukha Das Receptionist Initiated by Srila Prabhupada in 1974 in Atlanta USA, he lived in London for the last 6 years and presently serving at ISKCON London reception. Currently writing a book on Lord Siva’s importance in Krishna bhakti. Thirupati Gauranga das Govinda’s Restaurant Manager Our stalwart Restaurant Manager Thirupati has guided Govinda’s to ever greater heights. He pervades the temple with an ever increasing flow of prasadam to the delight of the devotees. Manjuali devi dasi Financial Controller, Charity Secretary Meet Manjuali, the vibrant guardian of balanced books! Driven by a passion for efficient administration, she ensures every moment counts in the noble pursuit of preaching among devotees. Jadavji Krishna’s Handyman A long term member of ISKCON London and known round the temple as “Jadu Bhai”. “Bhai” for his loyal & loving disposition, and “Jadu”, for his magical ability to build and repair all parts of the temple with a relentless smile. Aravind Krishna das Devotee Care He is shy by nature but once you know him, he loves having long casual chat. He is serving last 15 years along with his good wife Yamunesvari, taking care of devotees in East London and Essex area. Murli Manohara das Teacher and Preacher Since joined the temple in 1990, he has served in various positions including Head Pujari and is presently engaged in teaching Bhakti Sastri and other scriptural related seminars and classes as well as on line readings. Dhirodatta das Trustee, Mentor Resident Brahmacari, serving as a trustee with specialising in outreach & mentorship. If you see him, just say hello and you’ll be greeted with a warm smile and reassurance that Bhakti is for everyone! Krishnanandana Temple Commander Born into Krishna consciousness and educated at Mayapur Gurukul, he has long assisted with preaching at Studio 108. Now, he oversees temple operations, ensuring devotees’ wellbeing. Krishna Priya das Guest Greeter, Receptionist Whether it’s Prasadam in the mouth, an unforeseen glorification or his infamous hugs, Krishna Priya’s kind, caring and personally connected character consistently exhibits the perpetual love he has for everyone. Manjari Gopika devi dasi Festivals Coordinator An expert events organiser and always available to offer a helping hand, Manjari is always enthusiastic to serve the community and trains individuals in progressive Krishna consciousness with utmost care. Abhinandana Gopa das Studio 108 Manager, CVA Leader Having Krishna conscious roots at the Studio108, he is now manages this outreach project, driven by his passion for cultivating a sense of community, and introducing people to vedic wisdom. Rsi Raya Gauranga Das General Manager Another ISKCON London legend, Rsi embodies the courage of an Englishman and the compassion of a Bengali mother. His expertise and magical touch play a crucial role in maintaining and advancing the project. Devaki Devi Dasi Volunteer Co-ordinator Since 2010, Devaki has been inspired to engage and serve our volunteers, ensuring they feel cared for. She also oversees our Krishna Club for children, teaching and organising various children’s events. Radha Raman das Vice President, Trustee A long-standing dedicated member of ISKCON London who is also affectionately known as the minister of the interior, our vice president is always absorbed in thoughts of how to better serve the Vaishnavas. Yamunesvari Devi Dasi Devotee Care She and her husband organises weekly programs for Adults, Kids & Youth in East London & Essex. Famous for her nectar Krishna katha and joyful spirit, she is ever ready to assist anyone seeking devotee association. Dayal Mora das Temple President, Trustee A keen reader of Srila Prabhupada’s books and an enthusiastic chanter of Sri Harinam, Dayal Mora Das leads the temple with dedication and humility, serving the devotees with utmost care and sincerity. Acyuta Charan das Food For Life Co-ordinator Joined by his wife Krishna Kanta and twin daughters, Acyuta, a dedicated working professional, have been volunteering with FFL, distributing Prasadam to the needy in Central London for the last 17 years. Rupanuga Bhakti das Head Pujari, Trustee Often seen singing and dancing up and down Oxford street, Rupanuga Bhakti is also responsible for the day to day management of the temple and looking after the wellbeing of the devotees. Dipadatri Gaurangi dd LCVS Coordinator Joined KC through Pandava Sena in 2002. Works as an Asset Analyst at TFL London. A passionate volunteer organising retreats, facilitating courses, and contributing to the Bhakti Step mentorship program. Interested In Our Activities? pageText (crawler): Shanghai Me London | Asian Restaurant Mayfair Reservations Menu BUND Home menu bund Press What's on private dining Contact us Address 28th Floor, London Hilton on Park Lane, 22 Park Ln, London W1K 1BE Phone +44 20 4553 5888 Email reservations@shanghaime-london.com Socials Instagram legal Privacy policy A Modern Interpretation of Pan Asian Cuisine Shanghai Me merges the nostalgic charm of 1930s Shanghai with a playful modern twist, offering elevated Pan-Asian cuisine that is both authentic and inventive. Rooted in simplicity, quality ingredients, and the richness of cultural diversity, every dish tells a story. At its heart, Shanghai Me is dedicated to crafting moments of connection, joy, and celebration. Reservations Shanghai Me began its journey in the heart of Dubai, inspired by the elegance and energy of 1930s Shanghai. Shanghai Me began its journey in the heart of Dubai, inspired by the elegance and energy of 1930s Shanghai. Designed as a sanctuary for elevated Pan-Asian dining, it quickly became known for its vibrant flavours, refined service, and timeless Art Deco ambiance. Building on its success, the brand expanded to Doha, bringing the same distinctive experience to Qatar’s thriving culinary scene. Today, Shanghai Me enters a new chapter in London’s Mayfair, continuing its legacy of excellence with a seamless blend of tradition, innovation, and hospitality. From Dubai to Doha, and now London, our story is one of cultural celebration, crafted detail, and unforgettable. Our Menus With a wide-ranging menu blending tradition with modernity, Shanghai Me's menus encompasses the re-invention of thousand-year-old Chinese traditions married with Japanese techniques. pageTitle (crawler): China Tang | The iconic, art deco jewel in the Dorchester’s crown
pageDescription (crawler): China Tang is the epitome of art deco elegance, serving some of the best authentic Cantonese dishes in London
pageHeadings (crawler): About Us, RESTAURANT RESERVATIONS, PRIVATE DINING ENQUIRY, Get Social, Latest Instagram, New Years Eve
pageText (crawler): China Tang | The iconic, art deco jewel in the Dorchester’s crown BOOK View Menus China Tang at home Dim Sum by China Tang Harrods Restaurant The Bar Private Dining Rooms What’s on Gallery Gift Vouchers Contact Careers China Tang at home Dim Sum by China Tang Harrods Reservations View Menus About Us Mayfair restaurant, China Tang at The Dorchester, is widely heralded as home to some of the finest authentic Cantonese food outside China. Located within the iconic Dorchester hotel, the Chinese restaurant has built its steadfast reputation on serving a selection of deeply comforting Chinese favourites with style and flair. China Tang bears the unmistakable fingerprint of its flamboyant creator, Sir David Tang, dressed to impress even London’s most glamorous clientele. From the low-key Art Deco entrance on Park Lane, guests descend into a subterranean world inspired by the romance of pre-war Shanghai, where quirky objets d’arts twinkle in every corner and plush banquettes and ornate paneling flank the walls. The Bar at China Tang, a 1930’s-styled jewel and re-imagination of Sir David’s living room, welcomes you to experience our unique Cantonese afternoon tea, or to sample a selection of London’s finest libations and live music, in the heart of Central London. The dining room awaits, serving classical Cantonese dishes prepared in a time honoured manner, using the freshest ingredients to showcase centuries’ of perfected recipes. From the Dim Sum menus, to favourites such as Ma Po Tofu, Yu Xiang Aubergine and succulent Char Siu including the ultimate centrepiece, Peking Duck, bronzed, lacquered and juicy, we have designed a menu that celebrates Chinese cooking and excites the most discerning diner. Whatever the occasion, the team at China Tang are dedicated to delivering the highest quality of service, and an unforgettable experience to rival (almost) the company of Sir David himself. pageTitle (crawler): Ryoji Ikeda: Data.Cosm | 180 Studios Exhibition
pageDescription (crawler): Discover Ryoji Ikeda's Data.Cosm, an immersive digital art installation exploring data, sound, and mathematics at 180 Studios London.
pageHeadings (crawler): RYOJI IKEDADATA-COSM [n°1], Book Your Tickets, About Ryoji Ikeda, Visitor Information, Dates, Opening Hours, Location, CREATE, EXPERIENCE, EDITORIAL, LINKS, CREATE, EXPERIENCE, EDITORIAL, LINKS
pageText (crawler): Ryoji Ikeda: Data.Cosm | 180 Studios Exhibition CREATE EXPERIENCE RYOJI IKEDA DATA-COSM [n°1] Book Tickets Wednesday 15 October - Sunday 1 February Experience the world premiere of Ryoji Ikeda 's latest monumental installation - a total sensory encounter that transforms vast datasets into an immersive audiovisual environment, exploring the intersection of art and physics in unprecedented scale. Commissioned by 180 Studios, Ryoji Ikeda 's new site-specific audio-visual installation data-cosm [n°1] is a unique immersive experience charting the full spectrum of data on nature – from the microscopic scale of particle physics to the macroscopic of astrophysics, as was previously evident in the artist's data-verse project. data-cosm [n°1] functions as a giant microscope, a scientific apparatus, a clinical device, a monitoring system, and a window to an infinitely vast space. Visitors are invited to lie down on the floor and look up at the large LED screen set on the ceiling above them, their bodies surrounded by Ikeda 's soundscapes – a total sensory experience. ⚠️ Photosensitivity Warning: This exhibition features intense lights, high-frequency sounds, and rapid video transitions which may cause strong sensory stimulation. Book Your Tickets Experience issues with the booking widget? Book directly through our ticketing platform About Ryoji Ikeda Japan's leading composer and visual artist Ryoji Ikeda focuses on the essential characteristics of sound itself and that of visuals as light by means of both mathematical precision and mathematical aesthetics. Born in 1965 in Gifu, Japan, Ikeda has gained a reputation as one of the few international artists working convincingly across both visual and sonic media. He elaborately orchestrates sound, visuals, materials, physical phenomena and mathematical notions into immersive live performances and installations. 180 Studios and The Vinyl Factory have been collaborating with Ikeda since 2015, when The Vinyl Factory staged the UK premiere of Ikeda 's audio-visual installation supersymmetry at Brewer Street Car Park in Soho, London. 180 Studios subsequently exhibited Ikeda 's iconic interactive work test pattern, drawing over 100,000 visitors to 180 The Strand in 2017. Four years later, 180 Studios commissioned and produced Ikeda 's largest-ever European exhibition, featuring over ten artworks, creating an unrivalled immersive experience across two floors of the building that drew both critical acclaim and record visitor numbers. Visitor Information Dates Wednesday 15 October - Sunday 1 February Closed 22 December - 7 January for the holiday period Opening Hours Wednesday - Friday 12 noon to 8pm (Last entry at 8pm) Saturday - Sunday 11am to 7pm (Last entry at 7pm) Location 6 Surrey Street London WC2R 2ND Timed Entry Entry slots are available every 30 minutes for up to 40 people. Please arrive on time for your scheduled slot. Queuing may be required if you arrive late to your slot or if the space is at capacity. Members go free — find out more about Exhibition Memberships Photosensitivity Warning This exhibition features intense lights, high-frequency sounds, and rapid video transitions, which may cause strong sensory stimulation. If you are sensitive to light or have photosensitive or auditory conditions, please take special care when viewing the exhibition. column2: They do shows committed to increasing the number of Palestinian voices and stories in the arts and media.
pageTitle (crawler): ABOUT — PALART COLLECTIVE
pageHeadings (crawler): We are a group of , committed to increasing the number of Palestinian voices and stories in the arts and media., The escalating attacks on Palestine across Gaza and the West Bank revealed unprecedented levels of cruelty against all aspects of Palestinian civilian life, including arts and culture., Most government in the US and Europe has also attempted to crack down on legitimate forms of protest and solidarity with Palestinians, and an attempt to vilify them., Yet the crisis we are going through has also showed the depth of solidarity with Palestine and the great unity between Palestinians globally., Our aims support Palestinian artists and to contribute to high-quality creative content on Palestine. More concretely, we seek to…, 01, Benefit Palestinian artists or artists focusing on Palestine, by facilitating and showcasing their work and connecting them with opportunities in the UK creative scene., 02, Coordinate artistic endeavours, whether physical or electronic, with a focus on long term impact, as well as ad hoc responses to situations of urgency., 03, Curate high quality artistic work and showcasing it at UK venues, for example through the PALART festival., Ensure that spaces for conscious artistic expression relating to Palestine are maintained and expand and pushback against growing censorship and exclusion of Palestinian narratives., 04, Sign up to our newsletter, PALART COLLECTIVE info@palartcollective.com
pageText (crawler): ABOUT — PALART COLLECTIVE 0 Skip to Content PALART COLLECTIVE SHOWS FESTIVALS PROJECTS NEWS ARCHIVE ABOUT CONTACT Open Menu Close Menu PALART COLLECTIVE SHOWS FESTIVALS PROJECTS NEWS ARCHIVE ABOUT CONTACT Open Menu Close Menu SHOWS FESTIVALS PROJECTS NEWS ARCHIVE ABOUT CONTACT We are a group of  committed to increasing the number of Palestinian voices and stories in the arts and media. The escalating attacks on Palestine across Gaza and the West Bank revealed unprecedented levels of cruelty against all aspects of Palestinian civilian life, including arts and culture. Most government in the US and Europe has also attempted to crack down on legitimate forms of protest and solidarity with Palestinians, and an attempt to vilify them. Yet the crisis we are going through has also showed the depth of solidarity with Palestine and the great unity between Palestinians globally. Our aims support Palestinian artists and to  contribute to high-quality creative content on Palestine. More concretely, we seek to… 01 Benefit Palestinian artists or artists focusing on Palestine, by facilitating and showcasing their work and connecting them with opportunities in the UK creative scene. 02 Coordinate artistic endeavours, whether physical or electronic, with a focus on long term impact, as well as ad hoc responses to situations of urgency. 03 Curate high quality artistic work and showcasing it at UK venues, for example through the PALART festival. Ensure that spaces for conscious artistic expression relating to Palestine are maintained and expand and pushback against growing censorship and exclusion of Palestinian narratives. 04 Sign up to our newsletter Sign up with your email address to receive news and updates. Email Address Sign Up Thank you! PALART COLLECTIVE info@palartcollective.com

Please try again or go back and check your details Back to start Previous Confirm Together we will end homelessness Crisis is the national charity for people experiencing homelessness. We help people directly out of homelessness and campaign for the changes needed to solve it altogether. We work directly with people experiencing or at risk of homelessness in 9 areas across England, Scotland and Wales. We provide practical one-to-one support to help people access benefits, healthcare services, employment opportunities, and more. Our main aim is to relieve the huge pressure of homelessness, by helping people find safe and affordable homes as quickly as possible. Learn more about our work We provided intensive, one-to-one support to 5,321 people to help end their homelessness in 2023-24. 4,355 volunteers generously supported us during Christmas, in our shops, our services and more. 28,000 actions taken by Crisis campaigners urging political parties to prioritise ending homelessness. Our strategy for ending homelessness How Crisis can support you Get support from Crisis If you are homeless or at risk of becoming homeless, we can help. We offer services at locations across Great Britain. Find help from Crisis Help someone who is experiencing homelessness If you have seen someone sleeping rough and would like to help, find out how you can provide support. Support someone rough sleeping Resources for practitioners If you are professional working in the homelessness sector, we have resources and research to support your work ending homelessness. Explore resources about homelessness Make a difference Donate Make a one off gift, or set up a monthly donation. You can also pay in money you've already raised. Donate today Campaign We campaign for the changes needed to end homelessness for good. Campaign with us Volunteer You are part of ending homelessness, one journey at a time. Pop into the vintage photo booth to capture the moment and finish the night with a festive feast at @thegermangym.⁠ ⁠ Find out what’s on and plan your visit via the link in our bio. Bought to you by @lowerstablestreetmarket and @shotengai_market, experience the enchanting fusion of Japanese culture and festive cheer at the heart of Coal Drops Yard. Browse a delightful array of pan-Asian food and drink, homeware, art and accessories, supporting small businesses and independent retailers. From tasty cuisine to a tasty tipple, it has the food and drinks selection for you. From Barrafina and Coal Office to more casual options, Morty & Bobs and The Drop. The restaurants all have charming heated terraces so you can dine outside or inside, year-round. On Lower Stable Street you'll find Hiden Curry, Okan, MATCHADO and more. Vauxhall City Farm is an education charity based in the heart of London Just minutes away from the Thames and Westminster, The Farm offers all the sights and smells of the countryside, attracting over 60,000 visitors each year, making it one of London’s hidden treasures. Our offer includes a range of educational, horticultural and recreational workshops, volunteering, guided tours, a cafe called The Old Dairy and other visitors experience of an urban farm. Free entry, donation suggestion of £4 welcome. Open Tuesday–Sunday 10.30am–4.00pm (last entry at 3.45pm). Vauxhall City Farm 165 Tyers Street London SE11 5HS SEE & DO Our animals Animal experiences Birthday parties The Old Dairy Café Our workshops Room hire Volunteer Garden 50th Anniversary Appeal UPCOMING EVENTS From our big annual Easter Egg-stravegana, Spooktacular Halloween, and Christmas Wonderland to Summer celebrations including our Pride Party, we have regular events all year round and have just introduced live sports in the Old Dairy Cafe so get game ready! View all events Sign up to our Farm Futures newsletter Latest news, events, educational programmes, gardening clubs for adults and much more! Name Email Subscribe School trips and workshops We offer a range of fun and engaging workshops suitable for all ages including Meet the Animals, Land to Lunchbox, Plant Life and Sustainability. Learn about our programmes Corporate team building A great way to get your team involved in the local community, work on team building, fulfill your CSR requirements, and take positive steps towards improving employee health and welfare by getting them out of the office and into the fresh air with activities such as working on the Farmyard with the animals, gardening, and farm maintenance. Find out more Support us There are many ways to support The Farm and each one truly makes a difference. Donating helps to keep the animals fed and The Farm running. Plus, local communities are positively impacted through The Farm’s unique educational, recreational, and therapeutic offerings. Donate Adopt an animal Leave a legacy Volunteer Some reviews from our happy farm yard visitors Alicia Wroblewska ★ ★ ★ ★ ★ Very cute, small and wholesome farm with goats (friendly) bunnies, chickens and guinea pigs. You can also buy food for the animals for £1. There is a very cute cafe too. I recommend it for all ages! Laura Benedikt ★ ★ ★ ★ ★ Wonderful place! We went there during our visit in London and i must say it was much more than i expected! I adore animals and booked 2 meet and greet experiences with the Alpacas and Goats! …The animals are well looked after, well fed and very very friendly! … For me it was a dream come true! Prisoner Education Trust ★ ★ ★ ★ ★ I just wanted to say a huge thank you to you all for being such an amazing venue for our staff awayday last Wednesday (PET). It was such a successful day and so much of that was down to you guys. You were all so welcoming and a special thank you to the lady who provided us with our delicious lunch and cake. If you’ll have us we will certainly be back. Unlimited health ★ ★ ★ ★ ★ I just wanted to thank you and your team for having us on Tuesday. Your team was very welcoming and accommodating, we really had a great time! We definitely will be booking the rooms again. Lillian Bayliss Technology School ★ ★ ★ ★ ★ Just wanted to get in touch to say thank you for such a good workshop this morning - the kids all loved it and came out feeling calm and happy and knowing some new facts about the animals. Farmer for a Day ★ ★ ★ ★ ★ Hi, Just a quick note to say thank you very much for a memorable and fun volunteering experience on Wednesday. A special thanks to Josh who looked after us so well. Frequently asked questions If you have another questions please visit our FAQ page. What animals are at Vauxhall City Farm? We have a wonderful selection of animals at The Farm including pigs, alpacas, goats, sheep, and an aviary of birds, chickens, turkeys, rabbits, guinea pigs. And let’s not forget Jessie, our farm cat who you will see wandering around or basking in the sun. Do you have to book a ticket? Groups of 10 and more people must contact us to book in advance and may be turned away at the gate, if they are not pre-booked or we cannot accommodate. School groups All school groups and nurseries must email education@vauxhallcityfarm.org ahead of their visit. Group prices Price includes: a self-guided tour pack and animal feed. 10–20 people £64.80 (inc VAT) 21–30+ people £103.20 (inc VAT) Please note: Under 1’s are not counted in the visitor count. Groups are not allowed to split when they visit The Farm to avoid payment. Organisation groups (e.g. Mum groups) who have invited separate families to The Farm must pay. Carers/ teachers are not included in paid visitor count but must be accounted for in booking. Are dogs allowed? Yes! Well behaved dogs are welcome. Please keep your dog on the lead at all times. They are welcome inside and outside of the café, and in our community garden. However, they are not permitted onto the farmyard. Please pick up any dog mess and place it in the bin. If a dog is continuously barking, or causing visitors distress, they will be asked to leave by a staff / volunteer. Water bowls are available outside of the café. Licensed guide dogs are welcome on the entire farm. Is there parking nearby? There is on-street, metered parking just outside The Farm. Please note that The Farm is inside the congestion charge zone. We recommend you visit by tube or bus. We are a short walk from Vauxhall underground, train or bus stations, a 10 minute walk from Oval underground station and 20 minutes walk from Waterloo. What facilities are here? We have an on site café, The Old Dairy, serving hot and cold drinks and snacks. We have two unisex toilets, one accessible with baby changing facilities, at the top end of The Farm. We have three unisex toilets, one accessible with baby changing, in our café. We have two rooms that can be hired for workshops, meetings, presentations, product launches and much more. Please check out our Room Hire page for details! Sign up to our Farm Futures newsletter Latest news, events, educational programmes, gardening clubs for adults and much more! pageText (crawler): Turning Earth Ceramics: Pottery Studios & Classes in London OUR STUDIOS Turning Earth Leyton Turning Earth Highgate Turning Earth Hoxton In Production Classes All Classes Leyton Classes Highgate Classes Hoxton Classes Camden ART CENTre Classes Private Lessons New gift vouchers New Student FAQ Membership Sign up contracts info About ABout us contact us INFO frequently asked questions Cancellation policy Health & Safety News BLOG Events BOOK A CLASS OUR STUDIOS Turning Earth Leyton Turning Earth Highgate Turning Earth Hoxton In PRODUCTION Classes All Classes Leyton Classes Highgate Classes Hoxton Classes Camden Art Centre Classes New PRIVATE LESSONS Gift Vouchers Student FAQ Membership Sign Up Contracts Info About About Us Contact Us INFO Frequently Asked Questions Cancellation Policy Health & Safety News Events Blog TURNING EARTH Turning Earth's pioneering ceramics studios are for everyone, from beginners to professionals, and can now be found across London in Hoxton, Leyton and Highgate. We offer pottery courses and open-access membership, "like gym membership for potters" Join a class Become a member Professional makers The home of community pottery, open to all. Discover the art of ceramics in a vibrant, beautiful studio. Whether you are just starting out or well used to clay on your hands, Turning Earth offers the space, the community and all the tools to create and unleash your ideas. Join us and start making today! Our studios HOXTON E2 HIGHGATE N6 LEYTON E10 IN PRODUCTION Explore our pottery classes Discover the joy of working with clay on one of our extensive ceramics courses. Whether you’re a beginner looking to get your hands messy for the first time or an experienced artist interested in refining your techniques, our courses offer something for everyone. In terms of pottery creativity, the sky's the limit; and clay is virtually limitless. Pottery Courses for All Skill Levels Beginner Ceramics : Learn the basics of pottery, including hand-building techniques, wheel-throwing, and glazing. Perfect for those new to the art, this course will give you the foundational skills to create your own unique pieces. Intermediate Ceramics : Already familiar with the basics? Take your skills to the next level by exploring more advanced techniques. This course focuses on refining your style and mastering complex forms. Book a pottery class Reinventing the maker-space Turning Earth has grown to encompass four sites in North and East London, including Hoxton, Leyton and most recently, Highgate, which is located just on the edge of Queen's and Highgate woods. The studio for our professional makers -In Production- is also located in the Argall Avenue industrial estate. The first of its kind in the UK, Turning Earth is also known for the scale and beauty of its spaces and the efficiency of its technical systems and firings, which have been studiously refined for over a decade. Each of the studios is an open, airy, hub that can accommodate hundreds of makers each month. The spaces are exceptionally clean, minimising the risk to makers' lungs from air-borne silica reflecting Turning Earth's commitment to health and safety standards. Turning Earth pioneered the open-access membership model in the UK. Launched in 2013 to bring to London model of community ceramics makerspace that had been developing in the US, and subsequently inspired many similar studios, both in this country and around the world. Join our studio community Become a Studio Member Whether you’re looking to master your craft, or just put your hands in some clay and make something, Turning Earth will allow you to work at a pace that suits you. Come whenever works best for you, whether your free time is on a Sunday afternoon, or a Thursday lunchtime, or if it changes from week to week. Immerse yourself in creativity and join a thriving community of artists by becoming a member of our ceramics studio. Regardless of whether you're a hobbyist, a seasoned potter, or somewhere in between, our membership offers everything you need to create, connect, and grow your skills. Learn more about membership Why practice ceramics? Practicing ceramics may just make you a happier person. Studies have shown the numerous mental health benefits of participating in craft, which - because it is both repetitively physical and involves completing manageable tasks - releases the “happy chemicals” of serotonin and dopamine in the brain. A lot of people find that making, like meditation, helps restore a sense of presence and mental balance. Pottery making is part of the evolutionary history of human beings. As hunter gatherers, we spent millennia digging it from riverbanks and shaping it into usable vessels. By following our innate desire to put our hands in the earth and mould it, we are using our physiology in a way that is continuous with our evolutionary development. Beyond this, ceramics fosters a deep connection to nature and the environment. Clay, as a natural material, reminds us of the Earth's processes and cycles, grounding us in a world that often feels disconnected. By working with this elemental material, we cultivate a sense of gratitude and respect for the natural world, creating a holistic experience that feeds both the mind and soul. After the McCallisters mistakenly leave for the airport without Kevin, he awakens to an empty house and assumes his wish to have no family has come true. But his excitement sours when he realizes that two con men (Joe Pesci, Daniel Stern) plan to rob the McCallister residence, and that he alone must protect the family home. Book 3:00 pm Other dates It's A Wonderful Life 1946 130mins USA (U) Family/Drama Screenings from both 35mm and Digital. 35mm performances will be noted next to the time. If there is no note next to the time, the performance is Digital. Beset with personal and professional problems, George Bailey finds his previously happy life falling apart around him on Christmas Eve. Seeing no way out, George considers suicide from the edge of a bridge ­ but Clarence, his guardian angel, intervenes and shows George what his beloved hometown of Bedford Falls would be like without him.    Sold out 3:15 pm 35mm Book 8:30 pm 35mm Other dates The Muppet Christmas Carol 1992 89mins USA (U) Musical / Comedy 'Tis the season for love, laughter, and one of the most cherished stories of all time! Join Kermit the Frog, Miss Piggy, and all the hilarious Muppets in this merry, magical version of Charles Dickens' classic tale. Academy Award® winner Michael Caine (Best Supporting Actor) gives a performance that's anything but "bah, humbug!" as greedy, penny-pinching Ebenezer Scrooge. One fateful Christmas Eve, Scrooge is visited by the Ghosts of Christmas Past, Present, and Future. Together with kind, humble Bob Cratchit (Kermit the Frog) and his family, the Spirits open Scrooge's eyes -- and his heart -- to the true meaning of Christmas. Filled with original music and dazzling special effects, THE MUPPET CHRISTMAS CAROL will become a holiday tradition your family will treasure all the days of the year. Featuring the film’s famous long-lost song ‘When Love is Gone’! ------ As always you can expect SING ALONG performances of this new version of THE MUPPET CHRISTMAS CAROL this Christmas - and now we don't need to pretend that we weren't going to just play the lost scene regardless! Performances will feature a Hosted Pre-Show with our very own Father Christmas, where you will be prepped on what to do throughout the film. Costumes aren't mandatory, but 'Tis the season, and we love seeing you dressed up in your favourite Christmas Jumpers! All this, and a few more surprises up our sleeves, makes this one of the must-see events this Christmas! If you're wanting to Sing Along with The Muppet Christmas Carol, simply book for the performances marked SING ALONG [it's next to the start time]. All other screenings will be regular performances, and nothing will be written next to the start time.  Sold out 5:45 pm Sing Along Other dates Elf 2003 97mins USA (PG) Comedy Buddy (Will Ferrell) was accidentally transported to the North Pole as a toddler and raised to adulthood among Santa's elves. Unable to shake the feeling that he doesn't fit in, the adult Buddy travels to New York, in full elf uniform, in search of his real father. As it happens, this is Walter Hobbs (James Caan), a cynical businessman. After a DNA test proves this, Walter reluctantly attempts to start a relationship with the childlike Buddy with increasingly chaotic results. Sold out 6:10 pm Other dates Die Hard [is a Christmas Movie] 1988 131mins USA (15) Christmas / Action New York City policeman John McClane (Bruce Willis) is visiting his estranged wife (Bonnie Bedelia) and two daughters on Christmas Eve. He joins her at a holiday party in the headquarters of the Japanese-owned business she works for. But the festivities are interrupted by a group of terrorists who take over the exclusive high-rise, and everyone in it. Very soon McClane realizes that there's no one to save the hostages -- but him. Find out more Our Neighbourhood A stone’s throw from Notting Hill, Holland Park and Portobello Road, Shepherd’s Bush has a rich history of music, theatre and film, with iconic venues dotted throughout, like the 1960s-built BBC television centre at White City. Full of buzzy restaurants, independent shops, eclectic markets and great pubs, it’s one of London’s most diverse and exciting areas to get out into and explore. Shepherd's Bush Market Over 100 years old, Shepherd’s Bush Market is one of West London’s most famous places to splash some cash on everything from fresh fruit to unique antiques. Our top picks? Osman’s Juice Bar inside, and Mr Falafel, which is just around the corner. Go shop Kyoto Gardens Save yourself a plane ticket to Japan with a stroll through the beautiful Kyoto Garden - a leafy oasis in the heart of Kensington's Holland Park. Orignally built to celebrate the 1992 Japan Festival in London, it's a thing of pure beauty. Take me there Next Door Records Add to your vinyl collection at Next Door Records, a hip hangout that serves up music, coffee and natural wine. By night, you'll find DJs spinning tunes, panel discussions, art installations, supper clubs and more! Take me there Couverture & The Garbstore Fashion fans can flex their credit cards at Couverture & The Garbstore in Notting Hill, where you'll find an eclectic edit of womenswear, menswear, accessories and homewares in a three-storey converted period townhouse. Go shop Portobello Road Walk in the footsteps of Julia Roberts and Hugh Grant down Portobello Road in Notting Hill, home to one of the world's most iconic markets. Makes stops at the record shop Rough Trade West, Le Petit Citron for dinner, vintage shopping at Found And Vision, and Napoli Gang by Big Mamma for pizza perfection. Explore Buns from home Follow your nose to cult Notting Hill bakery Buns From Home, where sweet treats are the order of the day - think creamy cheesecake with homemade mixed berry jam and almond crumble in a laminated croissant bun. Go eat Goldfinger Named after the design master behind their Grade II listed Brutalist home, Goldfinger craft beautiful wood furniture, while also running a charitable initiative, People’s Kitchen, that feeds vulnerable residents in the local community. Explore The Oak W12 For a slice of English pub culture, visit The Oak W12, where you can feast on wood-fired pizzas in a grand Victorian building. Take me there Bush Hall With past lives as a soup kitchen in WWII, a bingo hall and a rehearsal space for the likes of The Who, Adam Faith and Cliff RIchard in the late 50s and 60s, Bush Hall has seen it all! Go for comedy and cabaret shows and stay for the live sets from the hottest new artists. Go listen Our Neighbourhood A stone’s throw from Notting Hill, Holland Park and Portobello Road, Shepherd’s Bush has a rich history of music, theatre and film, with iconic venues dotted throughout, like the 1960s-built BBC television centre at White City. Full of buzzy restaurants, independent shops, eclectic markets and great pubs, it’s one of London’s most diverse and exciting areas to get out into and explore. Shepherd's Bush Market Over 100 years old, Shepherd’s Bush Market is one of West London’s most famous places to splash some cash on everything from fresh fruit to unique antiques. Our top picks? Osman’s Juice Bar inside, and Mr Falafel, which is just around the corner. Go shop Kyoto Gardens Save yourself a plane ticket to Japan with a stroll through the beautiful Kyoto Garden - a leafy oasis in the heart of Kensington's Holland Park. Orignally built to celebrate the 1992 Japan Festival in London, it's a thing of pure beauty. Take me there Next Door Records Add to your vinyl collection at Next Door Records, a hip hangout that serves up music, coffee and natural wine. By night, you'll find DJs spinning tunes, panel discussions, art installations, supper clubs and more! Take me there Couverture & The Garbstore Fashion fans can flex their credit cards at Couverture & The Garbstore in Notting Hill, where you'll find an eclectic edit of womenswear, menswear, accessories and homewares in a three-storey converted period townhouse. Go shop Portobello Road Walk in the footsteps of Julia Roberts and Hugh Grant down Portobello Road in Notting Hill, home to one of the world's most iconic markets. Makes stops at the record shop Rough Trade West, Le Petit Citron for dinner, vintage shopping at Found And Vision, and Napoli Gang by Big Mamma for pizza perfection. Explore Buns from home Follow your nose to cult Notting Hill bakery Buns From Home, where sweet treats are the order of the day - think creamy cheesecake with homemade mixed berry jam and almond crumble in a laminated croissant bun. Go eat Goldfinger Named after the design master behind their Grade II listed Brutalist home, Goldfinger craft beautiful wood furniture, while also running a charitable initiative, People’s Kitchen, that feeds vulnerable residents in the local community. Explore The Oak W12 For a slice of English pub culture, visit The Oak W12, where you can feast on wood-fired pizzas in a grand Victorian building. Take me there Bush Hall With past lives as a soup kitchen in WWII, a bingo hall and a rehearsal space for the likes of The Who, Adam Faith and Cliff RIchard in the late 50s and 60s, Bush Hall has seen it all! Go for comedy and cabaret shows and stay for the live sets from the hottest new artists. pageTitle (crawler): Films with Live Orchestra, Music Hire, Snowman Wallace & Gromit, Pride | Carrot Productions
pageDescription (crawler): Carrot Productions is proud to be specialists and world leaders in the creation and staging of film and orchestra performances. We collaborate with artists to create exciting new shows for children and families, always placing the orchestra at the forefront of the experience. In our partnerships and collaborations with some of Britain’s finest creative brands and characters - including Aardman and Penguin - our award-winning shows have been staged worldwide.
pageHeadings (crawler): We are world leaders in performing live music to film, The Big Christmas Chorus, Carrot Productions teams up with Classic FM's charity, Make Some Noise for The Big Christmas Chorus, Carrot Productions' premiere of Peter and the Wolf animated film for The Snowman Tour 2025, First ever 'Musical Storyland Live' at Aviva Studios, Carrot Productions' Schools and Education Programmes: Fantastic Fairy Tales, Images, Contact Us, Policies

We are world leaders in performing live music to film About us What's on The Snowman Tour 2025 Make it a Christmas to remember with a special screening of family-favourite, The Snowman, accompanied by a live orchestra. Aardman In Concert 50 Years of Wallace & Gromit, Shaun the Sheep and Friends. We proudly presents a landmark celebration of Aardman’s extraordinary world – bringing together iconic characters, much-loved films and the thrill of a live orchestra for an unforgettable cinematic experience. What's On Our shows are performed all year round in venues and settings across the UK and internationally. See where and when our shows are being performed. The Big Christmas Chorus Have you ever dreamed of singing with a live orchestra? This year, you can. During The Snowman Tour, we’re inviting audiences to join us in singing Walking in the Air at the end of each performance. ​ Each year, young soloists perform Howard Blake’s beautiful song with our orchestra, and last year we welcomed the public to join our first Walking in the Air flashmob. Now, in support of Classic FM’s charity Make Some Noise, we’re launching The Big Christmas Chorus — a chance for everyone to sing or sign together for a great cause. ​ You can rehearse with our orchestra using the video below, and learn the BSL signs with Sean Chandler, deaf trumpet player and CBeebies’ Musical Storyland star. Whether you sing, sign, or simply enjoy the moment, you’ll help create something magical this Christmas. Find out more 800 performances of our shows worldwide in 10 years 325k+ audience members watching our shows in total 80+ venues across the UK where we have performed 7000+   children from schools engage with our shows per year Carrot Productions teams up with Classic FM's charity, Make Some Noise for The Big Christmas Chorus Have you ever dreamed of singing with a live orchestra? This year, you can! We’re inviting everyone seeing The Snowman Tour 2025 to join us in a mass audience singalong of Walking in the Air at the end of each show, accompanied by our amazing orchestra. It’s a chance to raise your voices and raise funds for Classic FM's official charity Make Some Noise. Carrot Productions' premiere of Peter and the Wolf animated film for The Snowman Tour 2025 This year's The Snowman Tour will also see the world-premiere of a brand-new animation of Peter and the Wolf by animator and animation director, Tsotne Rusishvili, alongside Prokofiev's symphonic masterpiece, in a stunning new musical reimagining by Daniel Whibley. First ever 'Musical Storyland Live' at Aviva Studios The first ever live version of the hit CBeebies' show, Musical Storyland Live took place at Aviva Studios in Manchester, with all the music composed by our Artistic Director, Daniel Whibley. Carrot Productions' Schools and Education Programmes: Fantastic Fairy Tales Carrot Productions have just undertaken an exciting residence at Nether Hall School in Leicestershire - bringing bespoke performances of our Fantastic Fairy Tales show to the heart of Leicestershire special schools. © Copyright Carrot Productions Ltd. Welcome to Brixton Village, London’s most diverse and vibrant market. A dynamic destination combining food, drink, shopping, music and culture – built on heritage, diversity and community. EAT & DRINK From authentic global cuisine to experimental cocktail bars, explore a world of flavour in Brixton Village – home of London’s most vibrant food and drink. View SHOP From traditional traders who have served locals for over 30 years, to artisan bookstores, record shops and more – discover Brixton Village shopping. View EVENTS & CULTURE From art and performance, to music, dancing and talks – there’s always something happening in Brixton Village. Find out what’s next. View Visit Us Consisting of Granville Arcade and Market Row, Brixton Village is home to more than 100 independent traders. These include; restaurants, bars, cafes, fishmongers, butchers, green grocers, fashion boutiques, lifestyle and homeware shops. Each boasting their own story. Each representing a different part of the world. Brixton Village is unpredictable. Ever evolving. Energetic. A place you visit for true originality and raw authenticity. More info Interactive Map Events From live performances, all day parties and guest markets, to talks and exhibitions, there’s always something happening in Brixton Village. NN.07 & F/CE Sample Sale 3112 Views 5 Comments tomaszr Follow I've been going to these sales since December 2022 and this was the poorest one. JV stock had no footwear and coats and AllSaints stock had no wool coats or jackets, just leather or light fabric ones. Over two visits I bought nothing. AllSaints Warehouse Clearance Sale 7827 Views 26 Comments dani13 Follow Went today. No queue, well organised by Showcase as usual. There’s a good amount of stock but it’s quite repetitive, you will see the same items over and over again in all sizes. Bags are disappointing and mainly just the one very padded style. Quite a good selection of shoes but nothing that I wanted (size 5). Some lovely coats, many are 1 to 2k but with the 80% discount that’s pretty good. I nearly bought a light jacket as the label said £440 and I thought that was before the 80% discount, apparently that was the discounted price. It wasn’t worth that. Came away with only one sweatshirt (£79). Decent sale, but for me it really showed me how overpriced Marni is these days. Marni Sample Sale 3994 Views 16 Comments leilakhoja Follow Great sale, broadly speaking all the styles were there and in sizes 3-8. Bundle offer available eg two fists for 50, individually they were 40 Air & Grace Sample Sale 694 Views 4 Comments georgie93 Follow I’ve honestly lost count of how many “sample ” Joseph has squeezed into the Music Room this year. At this point, they’re happening so often they might as well hand out loyalty cards. This latest one — rebranded as a “warehouse sale,” which unintentionally feels like an admission of defeat — was the most tragic yet. The stock looked like it had survived a small apocalypse: pieces with holes, stains, missing buttons, and the sort of wrinkling that suggests they’ve been hibernating in a bin bag since 2014. Clothes were strewn across the floor like a bargain-bin earthquake had hit. The absence of proper changing rooms meant half the attendees were undressing in the middle of the store — a free-for-all fashion dystopia that no one asked for. Every Joseph store seems to be perpetually “on sale” these days, which gives off the vibe of a brand rummaging through the sofa cushions for loose cash. And then there’s the new “BEAN” jewellery and bags — which, I’m sorry, look like someone dropped a kidney bean on the floor and said, “Yes. Luxury.” I can’t see that name without hearing “Beans beans Heinz” on loop. It’s hard to watch a brand with such a solid history drift this far off course. In my personal opinion, the current leadership has taken Joseph’s original identity, shaken it vigorously, and launched it into the sun. Joseph Warehouse Clearance Sale 3632 Views 23 Comments sophia777 Follow I went yesterday 6pm. Didn't see any rtw in the £55 category. Tweed dresses £350. But shoes were the best £60-£100 Ralph & Russo Private Sale 1856 Views 11 Comments michaelajerman Follow Went late. Best bit of the sale was the sweaters and tops. Not the biggest selection as time was running out. Coats were grand but much more pricey. Great staff. Mirrors to see oneself. What more could anyone ask for?! Didn't buy anything - next time. Designer Menswear Sale 1459 Views 11 Comments Jilan Follow I missed my priority slot and got there at 10:30. All the Cleo’s and Margot’s had gone. Some people were still trying theirs on but mostly smallers sizes which had more choice. I got lucky as the team suddenly started bringing out more size 8/41 and I found 1 Margot in their restock. All the shoes are individual priced on the boxes. Rene Caovilla Sample Sale 1985 Views 12 Comments maramoo Follow Just for everyone going for Perfect Moment, they didnt have the delivery they were expecting so got next to nothing for adults. 1 black and white cardigan repeated and lots and lots of the same 2 sweatshirt co ords. Then some leg warmers and the rest was virtually all kidswear. JAKKE Sample Sale 1240 Views 10 Comments rae558 Follow It looked like a car boot sale of 2nd hand clothes; all clothes were crumpled, wrinkled, looked shabby and old. I did buy a summer dress for £18 which was way too expensive for what it is and which I now regret but no changing rooms there… Monsoon Accessorize Christmas Charity Sample Sale 1362 Views 18 Comments CHICMI Follow The times for this event have been updated! ❤️😘 Yves Salomon Private Sale 4394 Views 15 Comments rae558 Follow I bought some cosmetics from a Japanese brand called Suqqu - lipsticks for £10 and concealer for £14! And some gorgeous earrings from By Pariah for £200. Happy with my purchases Christmas Gifting Sale 2226 Views 8 Comments Shoppinglove Follow My favourite sale to stock up on Christmas and birthday gifts. There wasn’t as much stock as in previous sales but still a lot of amazing sets to be had. There were polo RL pjs which were not available at previous sales and the price of stock was unbeatable. Staff were lovely as always. Bring cash for extra discount Intimates Lingerie Sample & Stock Sale 1747 Views 9 Comments ally3 Follow Extra 25% off today. Unless you are of size 37 which accounted for at least 60% of stocks they had in the room, there weren’t much left for other sizes. Malone Souliers Sample Sale 3196 Views 25 Comments carlo Follow Unfortunately lots of stock is damaged/dirty, I’m assuming returns. Prices expensive - £100 for a Paul smith scarf in a broken box. Harvey Nichols Sample Sale 6652 Views 20 Comments youvehadhotdinners Follow There are pros and cons to going to a Paul Smith sale on the last day, one hour before closing - pros: you get some great bargains, cons: there’s not much choice. If you’re like me, and don’t care about fashion and trends, but care about quality, then this was a good sale. I managed to get 10 items for £168.80, mostly because of the 20% discount on 3 or more items. I got 6 T shirts and 4 shirts; nothing for me but a lot of Christmas presents so I am happy. Paul Smith Sample Sale 5494 Views 23 Comments jay22 Follow Dear Box and Anya Hindmarch Team, Thank you for hosting another wonderful sample sale before Christmas. I attended on the first day when the ticket system was in place, and I understand the sale had to finish a day early due to high demand. I wanted to share a small suggestion: in the past, when you held the sale in Central London (such as Covent Garden before COVID), it was extremely popular and sold out quickly. It would be fantastic if future sales could be held in both East London and Central London, making it more accessible for Central London customers as well. Thank you again, and I look forward to your next sale. Anya Hindmarch Sample Sale 8083 Views 52 Comments stevie758 Follow Went along today. Got a couple of great bargains. Could do with a mirror and a changing area. Staff really friendly. Great prices. J.Lindeberg Archive Sale 1983 Views 13 Comments Linda Farrow Follow The Linda Farrow Sample Sale is freshly restocked this morning. Great selection with up to 90% off! Linda Farrow Sample Sale 2951 Views 5 Comments samplenews Follow Not a long queue and the wait is minimum. The good old days of Erdem sample sale might be behind us. Even the reduced rack of clothing was pricey. More coats and blazers this time. Overall, I wasn't inspired to buy and I have attended pretty much every sample sale they have done in the last few years. Erdem Archive Sale 2436 Views 6 Comments Eliask Follow Very poor sample sale, a lot of the same old stock from last years sample sale, freezing cold premises, as usual prices have increased. Canali Private Sale 2503 Views 10 Comments georginaplant Follow Quality of stock: 5 Pricing: 5 Friendliness: 5 Overall: 5 Amazing prices, super lovely and helpful staff gorgeous pieces with a changing room for trying on Self Service Christmas Archive Sale 627 Views 1 Comments georginaplant Follow Buy 4 items, 5th one free. But you can’t try anything on, rails are packed lots of items don’t have lining some damaged, 1/5 wouldn’t recommend RIXO London Sample Sale on the Road 6574 Views 10 Comments lorel Follow Sale was a departure from previous years, increasingly being more expensive year on year. Some of clothes were ridiculously priced a sample with no indication of brand, size or material for £60. A plain Hobbs cardigan, no tag £60 when this can be brought for a bit more on Hobbs online. It was a mish mash sale, which is fine but the high prices and ticket price was simply a bit much. Not being able to take items room to room was inconvenient. If the prices were better would have brought more. The Times x Smart Works Fashion Sale 2583 Views 14 Comments MinaD Follow Every time I attend this sale, it feels like they’re just bringing out leftover stock from the previous one and even raising the prices. It’s not fair at all! It’s actually diminishing their brand and making it less likely for people to attend. They should only run these sales when they have a good amount of new stock! Emilia Wickstead Sample Sale 2882 Views 7 Comments hamish Follow Definitely worth a visit for some fantastic PJs for adults and kids. Marie Chantal Sample Sale 706 Views 3 Comments Kazzy Follow I booked the 7pm to 8pm slot for Tuesday night after work. I hurried from the West End and was there at 7:05pm only to be informed they were closed. I was not happy! I prefer the Showcase venue on Regent Street because they honour their time slots. I'm sorry this is my very first negative comment Hunter Sample Sale 5278 Views 14 Comments IIPbII Follow Big selections of RTWs. I haven't seen any shoes and accessories as advertised on the price list. Majority of items are Superdry Imperfections label and sizes are mostly M for men and UK10 for women. Superdry Sample Sale 2669 Views 6 Comments bella23 Follow Only summer. Seems to be same clothes as from last sale 6 months ago - I noticed a few new additions but just a handful Prices seem to be higher as well. Ton of short dresses. There are also of course lots of maxis - very very old season. Not many people. And also not well thought out - only 2 change rooms in the whole warehouse! Very dusty mirrors everywhere. Also Thursday they closed at 7, not 8! Staff are generally super helpful - I didn't find that this time. Overall not an amazing sale - but might be worth it if you don't own anything yet from Farm Rio. Have fun! FARM Rio Private Sale 8115 Views 23 Comments Be1645 Follow Went Thursday afternoon. Still loads of stock, especially in 8 and 10. Mainly dresses - perfect for wedding outfits next summer and lots of them a steal (£60 or less if samples). Lovely, helpful team, and quiet today (apparently very busy yesterday when a lot of jackets and tops went). You can change behind a small screen although only space for one at a time. Jane Autumn/Winter Sample Sale 2037 Views 9 Comments olgae Follow Lovely sale as usual. Got there about 10:30 and it was so quiet and peaceful. Loads of items and honestly, I was quite surprised at the prices. I thought they were really good. Of course don’t expect anything for £50, but they had some egg charms for about £700 + and the actual egg pendants started from about £1200. The prices really were 70% off. They did have “concept” pieces which were made from silver or brass, but not sure how much they were. I would say in the hundreds rather than thousand. I got this charm for just over £700. On website right now you can see it is £2400 Fabergé Sample Sale 1335 Views 2 Comments Chicmi is my go to when shopping and discovering new brands. The fun never ends :) - Summer - Shopping and friendship rolled into one! Shopping has never been so much fun! :-) <3 - Laura Embleton - Love this site, everyday something new. - Lattakiab - A community of like minded shoppers; exchanging information, ideas and friendships. - Shade - I stumbled across this best kept secret, oh boy, I'm converted. I am a number one CHICMI fan. - Tracey-Jane Beaumont - The favorite platform of the fashionistas. The community is awesome, friendly and supportive. - Audrey Borius - I wish I’d found Chicmi sooner! A great concept! I highly recommend Chicmi! - Roz Clarke - OH, WHY DID I NOT DISCOVER THIS SITE EARLIER. TOTALLY ADDICTED!!! - A Edwards - I just love this.This is my new way to shop. Definitely worth signing up..! - Merilyn Hammond - a friend recommended Chicmi and I'm glad they did. Fun sale, fantastic clothes, fab service. - Joseph De Lappe - I love chatting with other people while I shop! - Grace - The experience was truly refreshing. - Zo - I use this site all the time. A great Place to try unknown brands. Highly recommend. - Maria Dormidontova - CHICMI FAN! They are my go-to for LA sales! - Sonja Wright - Chicmi is a great site with many different designers. - Sue Tomlinson - Absolutely loved the experience with this website. - Irina - What a wonderful way to shop! The set up is brilliant and deserving of 5* - Denise Crammer - Been with Chicmi for a few years. See the impact on your visit Posted More notices… Getting here Welcome to Crystal Palace Park, one of the country’s most important and historic green spaces. It is well served by public transport, with multiple rail and bus routes and numerous points of entry. Things to do and see Size up the dinosaurs, ride the skatepark, lose yourself in the maze… there’s so much to do in the Park. pageTitle (crawler): Battersea Power Station
pageDescription (crawler): Discover London’s most exciting new shopping and leisure destination at the iconic Grade II* listed, Battersea Power Station.
pageHeadings (crawler): CHRISTMAS, SHOP, EAT & DRINK, SEE & DO, WHAT'S ON, APARTMENTS, WORK
pageText (crawler): Battersea Power Station Search CONTACT | GETTING HERE | GUEST SERVICES Shop Eat & Drink See & Do All Family-Friendly Day Out Health & Wellness The Chimney Lift The Cinema In The Power Station Jurassic World: The Experience What’s On Events Inspiration Offers Apartments Apartments Overview New Apartments for Sale Apartments to Rent Re-Sales Request a Valuation Work Leasing The Engine Room About WELCOME TO BATTERSEA POWER STATION × Search… SHOP EAT & DRINK SEE & DO WHAT’S ON APARTMENTS Discover London’s most exciting new shopping and leisure destination at the iconic Grade II* listed, Battersea Power Station. Plan your visit here. This legendary London landmark and surrounding area has been brought back to life as one of the most exciting and innovative mixed use neighbourhoods in the world – a place for locals, tourists and residents to enjoy a unique blend of over 150 shops, bars, restaurants, leisure and entertainment venues, parks and historical spaces. A place to shop, eat, drink, live, work and play; here life doesn’t feel ordinary, it feels extraordinary. Opening Hours 27th November – 30th December: 10:00am – 9:00pm (Normal Sunday hours: 12pm – 6pm) 24th December: 9:00am – 5:00pm (Restaurant and leisure venues open later – please check with them directly for specific hours) 25th December: Closed (Selected restaurants open for Christmas lunch only – please check with them directly for specific hours) 26th December: 10:00am – 9:00pm 31st December: 9:00am – 10:00am (Restaurant and leisure venues open later – please check with them directly for specific hours) From the 1st January, the shops inside the Power Station will revert back to normal opening hours from 10am to 8pm Monday – Saturday and 12pm to 6pm on Sunday. Cafes, bars, restaurants, The Chimney Lift and the Cinema are often open earlier and later, please check the individual retailer pages for specific opening hours. CHRISTMAS It's Unmistakably Christmas at Battersea Power Station, with ice skating at Glide, unmissable view of London from The Chimney Lift and mouth-watering Christmas menus throughout the Power Station, plus all your Christmas shopping wrapped up in one place. FIND OUT MORE SHOP The new shopping destination inside the Power Station and along the grand pedestrian high street, Electric Boulevard, brings together an eclectic mix of high-end fashion brands, bars, restaurants, leisure and entertainment venues, complementing the existing offering at Circus West Village. DISCOVER MORE EAT & DRINK Dine around the world with restaurants specialising in an array of cuisines. From brunch, seafood and bottomless pizza to wine bars, breweries and sunset cocktails on the riverfront, Battersea Power Station is home to a wide range of culinary delights. DISCOVER MORE SEE & DO The Chimney Lift at Battersea Power Station is a one-of-a-kind experience, taking you to the top of the North West chimney for unrivalled 360-degree views of London’s skyline.
